items.title: European Equity Strategy: A Threefold Look at Earnings Revisions
items.summary: Recent improvements in our Europe ex-UK Earnings Revisions Index spotlight that earnings revisions are uncommonly high, unusually broad, and unseasonally strong. Historically, this setup has led to further earnings upgrades, improving forward EPS expectations, and solid equity returns.

items.title: Stock Market Country Selection:  Favoring Emerging Markets
items.summary: Model selection remains skewed toward Emerging Markets, with the regional framework retaining a cautious stance on the U.S. and Europe ex-UK while maintaining positive signals for China and Latin America. We offer further analysis and look at most- and least-preferred markets herein.

items.title: PPI Details Look Benign for PCE Inflation
items.summary: Following the June CPI downside surprise, June PPI data were also softer than expected. We expect June core PCE to be the softest increase since March 2025 and take a July hike off the table, with similarly subdued inflation data over the summer pricing out the chance of hikes altogether.

items.title: China Economics: All Eyes on Incremental Policies After GDP Miss
items.summary: China's second-quarter GDP came in at 4.3% year over year, the lowest rate since 2023. We trim our full-year forecast to 4.6%. We expect incremental policies to drive a mild rebound, including a potential 10-basis-point rate cut as soon as this month and accelerated fiscal policy.

items.title: U.S. Biotech and Pharma: Second-Quarter Preview
items.summary: Biopharma has seen a recent rally as its traditionally defensive nature finally took hold after bouts of geopolitical instability. We expect largely robust second-quarter prints from our large-cap names, likely adding to recent momentum in the sector. We discuss further in this note.

items.title: We're Downgrading Great Wall Motor to Sell
items.summary: Great Wall Motor reported a second-quarter earnings miss, mainly due to a delay in a Russia scrap tax rebate. Given repeated delays with this rebate, we see its receipt as unlikely and cut our 2026-28 earnings forecasts. We're downgrading GWM's H shares to Sell from Buy.

items.title: North America Gaming: Prediction Markets Face Court Losses
items.summary: Following Kalshi's preliminary-injunction loss in New York, we discussed the regulatory environment for prediction markets with expert Daniel Wallach. In this note, we discuss the potential impact of legal losses by Kalshi, as well as the potential opportunities for prediction markets.

items.title: IBM Offers Rare Negative Pre-Announcement
items.summary: IBM issued a rare negative preannouncement, warning of a stark revenue miss driven by large deal delays. Customer capex shifted to servers, storage and memory, weighing on Z-mainframe demand and cannibalizing software spending, especially in Transaction Processing.

items.title: U.S. Economics: We Think Softer Core CPI Is Just the Start
items.summary: Core CPI was essentially flat in June, softer than consensus expectations. Surprisingly cool June CPI, slowing in core PCE, and softer jobs data all but rule out a July rate hike; we expect similar data in coming months will lead markets to price out the chance of rate hikes altogether.

items.title: China Economics: AI Supercycle Powers a Structural Export Upswing
items.summary: China’s trade growth surprised sharply to the upside in June, driven primarily by robust demand for AI hardware and surging semiconductor prices, while the global energy transition continued to bolster green-tech shipments. We see this export upswing as becoming increasingly structural.

items.title: Is This the End of BMW's China Dream?
items.summary: BMW's very weak second-quarter unit sales were driven by a 28% year-over-year decline in China sales. BMW has been vocal about expecting a "stabilization" in its China business, but we see little evidence of that. In this note, we look at what may lie ahead and pose some key questions.

items.title: Oil Monitor: U.S.-Iran Tensions Escalate to Renewed Confrontation
items.summary: U.S.-Iran tensions over the Strait of Hormuz have escalated to renewed military confrontation, with President Trump announcing re-imposition of a blockade and other measures. Our base case is this renewed confrontation will stop short of mutual energy and public-infrastructure destruction.

items.title: Taiwan Electronic Components & Equipment: Investors' Questions
items.summary: Investor feedback has generally been constructive following our Dynapack initiation and BBU sector report, with Dynapack viewed as an interesting stock idea and most investors interested in the BBU theme. We highlight several key questions we've received and offer our answers.

items.title: U.S. Oil & Gas Equipment and Services: Second-Quarter Preview
items.summary: Oilfield-services stocks are contending with the on-off nature of the U.S.-Iran conflict, with the uncertainty making it difficult for investors to re-engage. But we see opportunities and seek to identify investment narratives with the best risk/return skew, as detailed in this note.

items.title: Global Gas and Power Insights: Henry Hub Price Premium to Erode
items.summary: U.S. Henry Hub prices are more likely to fall in the weeks to come. We offer an analysis of price levels, cash price behavior and, in particular, the December-January and January-February price spreads. We estimate that Jan ’27 prices look to have among the most premium that can be eroded.

items.title: U.S. Beverages and Household & Personal Care: Earnings Preview
items.summary: We expect muted trends in the U.S. and Europe given the impact of higher gas/energy prices on consumer sentiment, net of stronger trends in emerging markets. On the margin side, the recent decline in oil prices provides more flexibility to the group, but we also note upcoming headwinds.

items.title: Equity Markets Positioning Model: Positioning Divergence Widens
items.summary: Global equity positioning remains highly fragmented, with meaningful divergence across regions. In the U.S., recent gains haven't been matched by stronger positioning, suggesting limited conviction; we also see weakening positioning momentum across technology-sensitive markets.

items.title: Global Equity Quarterly: Back to the Broadening Debate
items.summary: Global equities have climbed ~10% year to date, though leadership has been concentrated in Tech. Many of our conversations are returning to the likelihood of “broadening” performance in the second half. We look at what's needed for broadening to succeed and discuss strategies herein.

items.title: Apple Preview: Pricing, AI and iPhone Launch in Focus
items.summary: We're constructive on AAPL and expect continued market share gains despite a slowing devices market. Selective price increases should support margins, while Apple Intelligence and Siri enhancements may boost engagement and services growth. The September iPhone launch is a key catalyst.

items.title: U.S. Autos: Positive 2Q Outlook
items.summary: Auto stocks underperformed in 2Q, but North America (NA) sales, pricing and production were better than expected, with favorable dealer inventory. While Europe and China remained weaker, trends met expectations. Low expectations and stronger NA results support a positive 2Q earnings setup.

items.title: U.S. Industrials 2Q Preview: Data Centers Still In Focus, Broadening In Sight
items.summary: We expect AI and data center demand, coupled with a broadening short-cycle recovery, to support solid 2Q earnings and accelerating organic growth in 2026. Following a sector pullback, we see more attractive entry points in favored names and open a positive Catalyst Watch on ITW.

items.title: U.S. Components & Hardware: Agentic AI Inflection Supports Storage and Optical Momentum
items.summary: Strong AI-driven demand continues to support storage, hardware OEMs and networking infrastructure. Storage remains the highest-conviction area given favorable supply-demand fundamentals, while agentic AI is driving demand for dense systems and supporting optical and networking growth.

items.title: U.S. Machinery 2Q Earnings Preview: Let the Good Times Roll?
items.summary: Machinery stocks have outperformed YTD, raising the bar for 2Q results. We expect most companies to beat, raise or at least maintain guidance as improving construction and truck demand offsets macro uncertainty. Recent policy clarity also supports higher estimates for commercial vehicles.

items.title: U.S. Economics Weekly: Shifting Balance of Risks
items.summary: Falling oil prices, softer inflation data and lower core PCE revisions should ease inflation concerns.  Softer labor market data in coming months should renew Fed officials and market focus on downside risk to employment. This should lead rate markets to price out hikes and price in cuts.

items.title: U.S. Rates Weekly: Red Card for Rate Hikes
items.summary: We remain received in the July FOMC meeting with a front-end flattener hedge as CPI should be supportive, combined with our view on seasonal jobs weakness. We look at the implications of this backdrop and consider what a lack of forward guidance will do to rates pricing.

items.title: Global Macro Strategy: Themes Into the Second Half
items.summary: While the latest Iran headlines are likely a fade, we took profits in assets most directly linked to oil prices. Chair Warsh has moved away from forward guidance, but Fed pressers remain informative. We explore other themes we see as notable, from earnings season to midterm elections.

items.title: PULSE Monitor: Threading The Needle
items.summary: We continue to expect a solid reporting season, but note that investor expectations are high. So here's the needle to thread: solid earnings reports, but with macro data that take the edge off of inflation concerns so that the market gradually prices out Fed rate hikes. Hot summer, anyone?

items.title: We're Downgrading PepsiCo to Neutral
items.summary: We're downgrading PepsiCo to Neutral given continued North America weakness at PFNA and PBNA. We think the current dynamic also creates carryover risk to numbers in 2027, when PEP will be cycling 2026’s highly active innovation period, with still-elevated cost inflation pressuring margins.

items.title: Artificial Intelligence: Models, Robots and Captives
items.summary: After the release of three models from frontier model providers, average output token prices continued to climb while GPU rental prices rose across Hopper and Blackwell instances. Both pricing trends reflect constrained supply; as explored herein, we expect this scarcity to continue.

items.title: Mining Equipment: Rock Solid Growth, Greenfield Outlook Improving
items.summary: Our Global Mining Equipment Survey expects robust growth in 2027-28, with greenfield intentions increasing and now also spread across later years. We see risk/reward as particularly attractive for Buy-rated Weir. Upstream mining is also showing strength, which should help Epiroc and CAT.

items.title: North America Chemicals: Examining Investors' Expectations
items.summary: A dynamic U.S.-Iran situation has re-opened the debate about the sector's relative beneficiaries. Paint & coatings names have been seen as gaining from price/cost expansion with easing inflation likely to show up in earnings exiting 2026, but conflict re-escalation complicates visibility.

items.title: U.S. Aerospace & Defense: Second-Quarter Industry Pulse
items.summary: We worked with Citi Research's Innovation Lab to conduct our third Industry Pulse supplier survey, seeking a better sense of industry volume, price, and margin trends in the second quarter as well as forecasts for the third. We summarize qualitative feedback from the survey in this note.

items.title: European Software and IT Services: A Trade Reversal Is Needed
items.summary: Despite steady to somewhat better updates in recent quarters, investors are waiting for catalysts for a trade reversal, with a broader sector de-rating having limited room for relative outperformance. We preview what we expect from SAP, Dassault, Sage and CAPP.

items.title: Waste Services: Second-Quarter Preview
items.summary: We see upside risk from higher recycled commodity prices and incremental M&A completed in the first half. RSG becomes our top waste-services pick, for reasons explored herein. The group has outperformed recently but still trails the S&P 500 year to date, and valuation looks reasonable.

items.title: Mexico Economics Weekly: Relief for the Inflation Outlook
items.summary: We're lowering our year-end headline and core inflation forecasts for Mexico. We've seen better-than-expected evolution of core inflation, supported by core goods inflation amid a resilient currency, while services inflation has continued to slowly ease despite the slack in the economy.

items.title: European Rates Weekly: Back to Brent
items.summary: The fragility of the U.S.-Iran ceasefire could seal a second ECB hike, but the UK is likely to stand pat. We explore our strategic recommendations and key themes, including Bunds looking like they have switched from rich to cheap in just over a week.

items.title: China Economics: Austerity Now, Acceleration Later?
items.summary: An underappreciated driver of China's sharp second-quarter slowdown is de facto fiscal austerity, with central and local governments each playing a role via distinct channels. Our base case is for a second-half reversal as fiscal deployment accelerates, driving a mild rebound.

items.title: China Economics: An Inflation Plateau?
items.summary: China's CPI and PPI were narrowly below expectations in June, but that was mostly due to external factors including oil and gold prices. We expect a plateau for inflation in the second half, with the upcoming Politburo meeting possibly offering more signals about fiscal policy.

items.title: Global Metals & Mining: Second-Quarter Previews
items.summary: Our expectations for the quarter are for a modest operational recovery only, as we think production will remain skewed toward the second half. We expect a broader trend of guidance fine-tuning to play out with higher energy prices. We offer further analysis in this note.

items.title: Addressing Key Netflix Debates Ahead of Second-Quarter Results
items.summary: Sentiment on Netflix remains subdued for four reasons: tepid engagement, fears of M&A, a lack of catalysts, and enthusiasm for semis. But we remain more upbeat: We find the valuation compelling, believe M&A could be positive, and see new tiers as a potential positive catalyst.

items.title: We're Initiating Coverage of Chery at Buy
items.summary: We're initiating coverage of Chery at Buy with a target price of HK$36. Chery is China's third-largest auto maker and the leader in terms of exports. We see the sector at a historical cyclical bottom in July and note several other reasons to support our Buy rating.

items.title: Emerging Markets Strategy Weekly: Monthly EM Bond Portfolio
items.summary: We review the implications of the latest U.S.-Iran events and note that recent geopolitical developments mark a good moment for further down recalibration in risky positions, especially among the EM liquid positions. We offer further analysis and suggested strategies in this note.

items.title: Citi CIO Survey: IT Budgets Improve As AI Becomes Bigger Priority
items.summary: Our second-quarter CIO survey suggests an improved spending backdrop with IT budgets accelerating as AI continues to gain as an investment priority. Data/AI remained the top priority, followed by cybersecurity, digital transformation and customer-facing applications.

items.title: U.S. Software: Key Themes for Earnings Season
items.summary: A number of recent signs point to an improved spending backdrop, but we'd warn against extrapolating these recent improvements to conclude there's a broad-based recovery across software. We continue to favor names most directly levered to enterprise AI adoption and infrastructure buildout.

items.title: North America Transportation: Earnings Preview
items.summary: Second-quarter earnings are likely to be among the strongest in years, as transportation names benefit from significantly tighter capacity conditions coupled with moderately improving demand. We move KNX and SAIA back to Buy and ODFL back to Neutral following a recent pullback in shares.

items.title: India Equity Strategy: What to Look for During Earnings Season
items.summary: With inflationary pressures ebbing, messaging about near-term growth and profitability may be more important than Indian names' earnings numbers. Equities remain relatively reasonable, consensus earnings estimates have held up well so far, and drivers and positioning still look favorable.

items.title: Insights From Our Space & AI Virtual Teach-In
items.summary: Our inaugural Space & AI Virtual Day framed the scale of the opportunity at these two sectors' intersection; the benefits of vertical integration; and the need for investment across power, semiconductors, compute, models, software and distribution. We offer key takeaways in this note.

items.title: China Cosmetics Survey Finds Resilient But Polarized Demand
items.summary: Our China cosmetics survey found resilient industry demand, but consumption is becoming polarized: High-spending consumers in top-tier cities are trading up, while younger and lower-tier city consumers show weaker sentiment and greater price sensitivity. We review preferred names herein.

items.title: Africa Economics & Strategy Weekly: Notes on Gabon and Senegal
items.summary: We consider the evolution of economic policy in Gabon and how it could dovetail with a possible IMF program. We also think about how politics could evolve in Senegal and the potential implications for resolving the debt problem currently hanging over the country.
